nodes.title: Managing security risk
nodes.raw_text: Appropriate organisational structures, policies, processes and procedures in place to understand, assess and systematically manage security risks to network and information systems supporting essential functions.

nodes.title: Governance
nodes.raw_text: The organisation has appropriate management policies, processes and procedures in place to govern its approach to the security of network and information systems.

nodes.title: Board Direction
nodes.raw_text: You have effective organisational security management led at board level and articulated clearly in corresponding policies.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: board-level leadership and clearly articulated policies for the security of network and information systems; in order to: govern and direct protection of the essential function.

nodes.title: Roles and Responsibilities
nodes.raw_text: Your organisation has established roles and responsibilities for the security of network and information systems at all levels, with clear and well-understood channels for communicating and escalating risks.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: defined security roles and responsibilities at all levels, with clear channels to communicate and escalate risk; in order to: ensure accountability for protecting network and information systems.

nodes.title: Decision-making
nodes.raw_text: You have senior-level accountability for the security of network and information systems, and delegate decision-making authority appropriately and effectively. Risks to network and information systems related to the operation of your essential function(s) are considered in the context of other organisational risks.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: senior accountability and appropriately delegated authority for security decisions, with cyber risk weighed alongside other organisational risks; in order to: make informed, prioritised risk decisions.

nodes.title: Risk Management
nodes.raw_text: The organisation takes appropriate steps to identify, assess and understand security risks to network and information systems supporting the operation of essential functions. This includes an overall organisational approach to risk management.

nodes.title: Risk Management Process
nodes.raw_text: Your organisation has effective internal processes for managing risks to the security and resilience of network and information systems related to the operation of your essential function(s) and communicating associated activities.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: effective internal processes to identify, assess and manage security and resilience risks to essential-function systems, and to communicate them; in order to: keep risk within acceptable bounds.

nodes.title: Understanding Threat
nodes.raw_text: You understand the capabilities, methods and techniques of threat actors and what network and information systems they may compromise to adversely impact your essential function(s). This information is used to inform security and resilience risk management decisions, adjusting, enhancing or adding security measures to better defend against threats.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: understanding of threat actors' capabilities and methods, used to adjust and add security measures; in order to: defend essential-function systems against relevant threats.

nodes.title: Assurance
nodes.raw_text: You have gained confidence in the effectiveness of the security of your technology, people, and processes relevant to the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: justified confidence that the security of technology, people and processes is effective; in order to: assure protection of essential-function systems.

nodes.title: Asset Management
nodes.raw_text: Everything required to deliver, maintain or support network and information systems necessary for the operation of essential functions is determined and understood. This includes data, people and systems, as well as any supporting infrastructure (such as power or cooling).

nodes.title: Asset Management
nodes.raw_text: Asset Management
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: identifying and understanding all assets, including systems, data, people and dependencies, that support essential functions; in order to: manage and protect them effectively.

nodes.title: Supply Chain
nodes.raw_text: The organisation understands and manages security risks to network and information systems supporting the operation of essential functions that arise as a result of dependencies on suppliers. This includes ensuring that appropriate measures are employed where third party services are used.

nodes.title: Supply Chain
nodes.raw_text: You understand and effectively manage the risks associated with suppliers to the security of network and information systems supporting the operation of your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: understanding and managing the security risks that suppliers pose to essential-function systems; in order to: prevent supplier-introduced compromise.

nodes.title: Secure Software Development and Support
nodes.raw_text: You actively maximise the use of secure and supported software, whether developed internally or sourced externally, within network and information systems supporting the operation of your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: maximising use of secure and supported software, whether built or sourced externally; in order to: reduce vulnerabilities in essential-function systems.

nodes.title: Protecting against cyber attack
nodes.raw_text: Proportionate security measures are in place to protect network and information systems supporting essential functions from cyber attack.

nodes.title: Service Protection Policies, Processes and Procedures
nodes.raw_text: The organisation defines, implements, communicates and enforces appropriate policies, processes and procedures that direct its overall approach to securing systems and data that support operation of essential functions.

nodes.title: Policy, Process and Procedure Development
nodes.raw_text: You have developed and continue to improve a set of cyber security and resilience policies, processes and procedures that manage and mitigate the risk of adverse impact to network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: developing and continually improving cyber security and resilience policies, processes and procedures; in order to: manage and mitigate risk to essential-function systems.

nodes.title: Policy, Process and Procedure Implementation
nodes.raw_text: You have successfully implemented your security policies, processes and procedures and can demonstrate the security benefits achieved.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: implementing security policies, processes and procedures and demonstrating the security benefits achieved; in order to: realise intended protection in practice.

nodes.title: Identity and Access Control
nodes.raw_text: The organisation understands, documents and manages access to network and information systems supporting the operation of essential functions. Users (or automated functions) that can access data or systems are appropriately verified, authenticated and authorised.

nodes.title: Identity Verification, Authentication and Authorisation
nodes.raw_text: You robustly verify, authenticate and authorise access to network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: robust verification, authentication and authorisation of access to essential-function systems; in order to: ensure only legitimate access.

nodes.title: Device Management
nodes.raw_text: You fully know and have trust in the devices that are used to access your networks, information systems and data that support your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: knowing and trusting the devices used to access networks, systems and data; in order to: prevent access from untrusted or compromised devices.

nodes.title: Privileged User Management
nodes.raw_text: You closely manage privileged user access to network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: close management of privileged user access to essential-function systems; in order to: limit misuse of elevated privileges.

nodes.title: Identity and Access Management (IdAM)
nodes.raw_text: You closely manage and maintain identity and access control for users, devices and systems accessing network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: managing and maintaining identity and access control for users, devices and systems; in order to: keep access appropriate over time.

nodes.title: Data Security
nodes.raw_text: Data stored or transmitted electronically is protected from actions such as unauthorised access, modification, or deletion that may cause an adverse impact on essential functions. Such protection extends to the means by which authorised users, devices and systems access critical data necessary for the operation of essential functions. It also covers information that would assist a threat actor, such as design details of network and information systems.

nodes.title: Understanding Data
nodes.raw_text: You have a good understanding of data important to the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s), where it is stored, where it travels and how unavailability or unauthorised access, uncontrolled release, modification or deletion would adversely impact the essential function(s). This also applies to third parties storing or accessing data important to the operation of your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: understanding important data, where it is stored and transits, and the impact of its loss or compromise, including via third parties; in order to: protect it appropriately.

nodes.title: Data in Transit
nodes.raw_text: You have protected the transit of data important to the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s). This includes the transfer of data to third parties.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: protecting data in transit, including transfers to third parties; in order to: preserve its confidentiality and integrity in motion.

nodes.title: Stored Data
nodes.raw_text: You have protected stored soft and hard copy data important to the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: protecting stored soft and hard copy data important to essential functions; in order to: preserve its confidentiality and integrity at rest.

nodes.title: Mobile Data
nodes.raw_text: You have protected data important to the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s) on mobile devices (e.g. smartphones, tablets and laptops).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: protecting important data held on mobile devices such as phones, tablets and laptops; in order to: prevent loss or compromise from mobile exposure.

nodes.title: Media / Equipment Sanitisation
nodes.raw_text: Before reuse and / or disposal you appropriately sanitise devices, equipment and removable media holding data important to the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: appropriately sanitising devices, equipment and removable media before reuse or disposal; in order to: prevent recovery of important data.

nodes.title: System Security
nodes.raw_text: Network and information systems and technology critical for the operation of essential functions are protected from cyber attack. An organisational understanding of risk to essential functions informs the use of robust and reliable protective security measures to effectively limit opportunities for threat actors to compromise networks and systems.

nodes.title: Secure by Design
nodes.raw_text: You design security into network and information systems that support the operation of your essential function(s). You minimise their attack surface and ensure that the operation of your essential function(s) should not be impacted by the exploitation of any single vulnerability.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: designing security into systems and minimising attack surface so no single vulnerability disrupts the essential function; in order to: limit exploitability and impact.

nodes.title: Secure Configuration
nodes.raw_text: You securely configure network and information systems that support the operation of your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: securely configuring essential-function network and information systems; in order to: reduce exposure from insecure settings.

nodes.title: Secure Management
nodes.raw_text: You manage your organisation's network and information systems that support the operation of your essential function(s) to enable and maintain security.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: managing network and information systems to enable and maintain security; in order to: sustain protection through operations.

nodes.title: Resilient Networks and Systems
nodes.raw_text: The organisation builds resilience against cyber attack and system failure into the design, implementation, operation and management of systems that support the operation of your essential function(s).

nodes.title: Resilience Preparation
nodes.raw_text: You are prepared to restore the operation of your essential function(s) following adverse impact to network and information systems.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: preparedness to restore essential-function operation after adverse impact to systems; in order to: ensure timely recovery.

nodes.title: Design for Resilience
nodes.raw_text: You design network and information systems supporting your essential function(s) to be resilient to cyber security incidents. Systems are appropriately segregated and resource limitations are mitigated.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: designing systems to be resilient, with appropriate segregation and mitigated resource limits; in order to: withstand and contain cyber incidents.

nodes.title: Backups
nodes.raw_text: You hold accessible and secured current backups of data and information needed to recover operation of your essential function(s) following an adverse impact to network and information systems.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: holding accessible, secured, current backups of the data and information needed to recover; in order to: restore the essential function after impact.

nodes.title: Staff Awareness and Training
nodes.raw_text: Staff have appropriate awareness, knowledge and skills to carry out their organisational roles effectively in relation to the security of network and information systems supporting the operation of your essential function(s).

nodes.title: Cyber Security Culture
nodes.raw_text: You develop and maintain a positive cyber security culture and a shared sense of responsibility.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: developing and maintaining a positive cyber security culture and shared responsibility; in order to: improve security behaviour across the organisation.

nodes.title: Cyber Security Training
nodes.raw_text: The people who support the operation of network and information systems supporting your essential function(s) are appropriately trained in cyber security.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: appropriately training the people who support essential-function systems in cyber security; in order to: equip staff to act securely.

nodes.title: Detecting Cyber Security Events
nodes.raw_text: Capabilities exist to ensure security defences remain effective and to detect cyber security events and incidents adversely affecting, or with the potential to adversely affect, essential function(s).

nodes.title: Security Monitoring
nodes.raw_text: The organisation monitors the security status of network and information systems supporting the operation of essential function(s) in order to detect security events indicative of a security incident.

nodes.title: Sources and Tools for Logging and Monitoring
nodes.raw_text: The data sources and tools that you include in your logging and monitoring allow for timely identification of events which might adversely affect the security or resiliency of network and information system(s) supporting the operation of your essential function(s).
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: logging and monitoring data sources and tools that enable timely identification of security-relevant events; in order to: detect threats to essential-function systems.

nodes.title: Securing Logs
nodes.raw_text: You hold log data securely and grant appropriate user and system access only to accounts with a business need. Log data is held for a suitable retention period, after which it is deleted.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: holding log data securely, restricting access to those with a business need, and retaining then deleting it appropriately; in order to: protect and govern log integrity.

nodes.title: Generating Alerts
nodes.raw_text: Evidence of potential security incidents contained in your monitoring data is reliably identified and where appropriate triggers alerts.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: reliably identifying evidence of potential incidents in monitoring data and triggering alerts where appropriate; in order to: surface incidents for response.

nodes.title: Triage of Security Alerts
nodes.raw_text: You contextualise alerts with knowledge of the threat and your systems, to identify security incidents as well as responding to all alerts appropriately.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: contextualising and triaging alerts using threat and system knowledge, and responding to all appropriately; in order to: identify and handle genuine incidents.

nodes.title: Personnel Skills for Monitoring and Detection
nodes.raw_text: Monitoring and detection personnel skills and roles, including those outsourced, reflect governance and reporting requirements, expected threats and the complexities of the network or system data they need to use. Monitoring and detection personnel have sufficient knowledge of network and information systems and the essential function(s) they need to protect.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: monitoring and detection personnel, including outsourced, with skills and knowledge matched to threats, systems and reporting needs; in order to: detect incidents competently.

nodes.title: Understanding User’s and System’s Behaviour, and Threat Intelligence (within Security Monitoring)
nodes.raw_text: Threats to the operation of network and information systems, and corresponding user and system behaviour, are sufficiently understood. These are used to detect cyber security incidents.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: sufficient understanding of threats and of normal user and system behaviour; in order to: detect cyber security incidents.

nodes.title: Threat Hunting
nodes.raw_text: The organisation proactively seeks to detect, within networks and information systems, adverse activity affecting, or with the potential to affect, the operation of essential functions even when the activity evades standard security prevent / detect solutions (or when standard solutions are not deployable).

nodes.title: Threat Hunting
nodes.raw_text: Threat Hunting
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: proactively searching systems and data for threats and indicators of compromise that evade existing detection; in order to: find incidents automated monitoring misses.

nodes.title: Minimising the impact of cyber security
nodes.raw_text: incidents Capabilities exist to minimise the adverse impact of a cyber security incident on the operation of essential functions, including the restoration of those function(s) where necessary.

nodes.title: Response and Recovery Planning
nodes.raw_text: There are well-defined and tested incident management processes in place, that aim to ensure continuity of essential function(s) in the event of system or service failure. Mitigation activities designed to contain or limit the impact of compromise are also in place.

nodes.title: Response Plan
nodes.raw_text: You have an up-to-date incident response plan that is grounded in a thorough risk assessment that takes account of network and information systems supporting the operation of your essential function(s) and covers a range of incident scenarios.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: an up-to-date, risk-based incident response plan covering a range of scenarios for essential-function systems; in order to: respond effectively to incidents.

nodes.title: Response and Recovery Capability
nodes.raw_text: You have the capability to enact your incident response plan, including effective limitation of impact on the operation of your essential function(s). During an incident, you have access to timely information on which to base your response decisions.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: capability to enact the response plan, limit impact, and access timely information during an incident; in order to: contain incidents and recover the essential function.

nodes.title: Testing and Exercising
nodes.raw_text: Your organisation carries out exercises to test response plans, using past incidents that affected your (and other) organisation, and scenarios that draw on threat intelligence and your risk assessment.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: exercising response plans using past incidents, threat intelligence and risk assessment; in order to: validate and improve readiness.

nodes.title: Lessons Learned
nodes.raw_text: When an incident occurs, steps are taken to understand its causes and to ensure remediating action is taken to protect against future incidents.

nodes.title: Post Incident Analysis
nodes.raw_text: When an incident occurs, your organisation takes steps to understand its causes, informing appropriate remediating action.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: analysing incidents to understand their causes and inform remediating action; in order to: prevent recurrence.

nodes.title: Using Incidents to Drive Improvements
nodes.raw_text: Your organisation uses lessons learned from incidents to improve your security measures.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: using lessons learned from incidents to improve security measures; in order to: strengthen defences over time.
